# Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın görev alanı ve teşkilatında hangi değişiklikler yapıldı? 
![Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın görev alanı dijital devlet ve yapay zekayı da kapayacak şekilde genişletildi](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/db44/live/bb3635d0-e12f-11f0-92d8-c5336946eff2.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, İletişim Başkanlığı
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın görev alanı dijital devlet ve yapay zekayı da kapayacak şekilde genişletildi**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer, Londra
  * 25 Aralık 2025, 04:21 +03
Güncelleme 4 saat önce
  * Okuma süresi: 2 dk


[_**Gündemi BBC Türkçe'den takip etmek artık WhatsApp'ta da mümkün. Haberlerimizin doğrudan telefonunuza gelmesi için tıklayın.**_](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c87lyvevwz8o)
**Resmi Gazete'de yayımlanan Cumhurbaşkanlığı Kararnamesi ile Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın yetki ve sorumluluklarında kapsamlı değişikliklere gidildi.**
192 sayılı Kararname ile Başkanlığın görev alanına dijital devlet ve kamuda yapay zeka başlıkları eklendi, teşkilat yapısı yeniden düzenlendi.
Kararnameye göre Başkanlık; siber güvenlik ve dijital devlet alanında mevzuat çalışmalarını yürütecek, ulusal strateji ve eylem planlarını hazırlayacak, uygulamaları koordine edecek. 
Dijital devlet kurumsal mimarisinin oluşturulması, kamu kurumlarının bilişim ürün ve sistemlerine ilişkin idari, mali ve teknik standartların belirlenmesi de Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın
[Reklam](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/kurumsal/2015/01/000000_advertising_faqs)
Yeni düzenlemeyle e-Devlet Kapısı ile dijital devlet ortak ürün ve hizmetlerinin geliştirilmesi ve işletilmesi, kamu bilişim projelerinde proje yönetim standartlarının belirlenmesi ve bu projelerin mali-teknik boyutlarına ilişkin Strateji ve Bütçe Başkanlığına görüş verilmesi öngörülüyor.
Kamuda yapay zeka uygulamalarına yönelik mevzuat çalışmaları, ulusal politika ve stratejilere katkı, veri yönetişimi ilke ve standartlarının belirlenmesi ile ortak veri alanı altyapısının hayata geçirilmesi de Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın görev kapsamına girdi.
## Yeni teşkilat yapısı
192 sayılı Cumhurbaşkanı Kararnamesi'yle Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığı'nın yapısı da yeniden şekillendirildi.

# Libya Genelkurmay Başkanı dahil 8 kişinin öldüğü uçak kazasıyla ilgili soruşturma ne aşamada?
![Uçağın enkazından bir parça bir tarlada görünüyor ve arka plandaki tepenin alt kısmında araçlar park etmiş halde duruyor](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/9870/live/7e127090-e0dc-11f0-94d6-33dfd766fcaa.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Enes Yıldırım/ Anadolu / Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Uçağın enkazından bir parça**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Unvan, Londra
  * 23 Aralık 2025
Güncelleme 24 Aralık 2025
  * Okuma süresi: 5 dk


**Türkiye'yi ziyaret eden Libya Genelkurmay Başkanı Orgeneral Muhammed Ali Ahmed Al Haddad, ülkesine dönüşte geçirdiği uçak kazasında hayatını kaybetti.**
Al Haddad dahil sekiz kişiyi taşıyan Falcon 50 tipi uçak, Ankara'dan Trablus'a gitmek üzere havalandıktan 19 dakika sonra Haymana ilçesinde düştü. 
Uçağın enkazına Esenboğa Havalimanı'na 105 kilometre uzaklıkta bulunan Ankara'nın Haymana ilçesine bağlı Kesikkavak köyü yakınlarında ulaşıldı.
Haddad dahil uçaktaki herkesin hayatını kaybettiğini, Libya Ulusal Birlik Hükümeti Başbakanı Abdulhamid Dibeybe açıkladı. 
[Reklam](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/kurumsal/2015/01/000000_advertising_faqs)
Uçakta Libya Genelkurmay Başkanı Al Haddad'ın yanı sıra Libya Kara Kuvvetleri Komutanı Korgeneral Futuri Gribel, Askeri İmalat Kurumu Komutanı Tuğgeneral Mahmud Al Katavi, Libya Genelkurmay Başkanı Danışmanı Muhammed Al Assavi Diyab ve Genelkurmay Başkanlığı Fotoğrafçısı Muhammed Ömer Ahmed Mahcub ve üç personel bulunuyordu.
Libya'da kaza nedeniyle üç günlük ulusal yas ilan edildi.
  * [Muhammed Ali Ahmed Al Haddad kimdir, Libya'da hangi görevlerde bulundu?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c4gp7m0wk1jo)

# 11. Yargı Paketi yürürlüğe girdi: Deprem suçları kapsam dışı bırakıldı 
!["Çocuklarımızın mezarı üstünden 27. Madde geçemez" yazılı döviz taşıyan bir kadın](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/301d/live/5646a870-e01d-11f0-a378-fd9ff3d870e9.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Adalet Peşinde Aileler Platformu / X
**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, Ayşe Sayın
    * Bildirdiği yer, Ankara
  * 23 Aralık 2025
Güncelleme bir saat önce
  * Okuma süresi: 3 dk


**11. Yargı Paketi TBMM'de kabul edildi. Resmi Gazete'de yayımlanarak yürülüğe giren paketle, deprem kaynaklı meydana gelen öldürme suçları düzenlemeden. çıkarıldı. "Terör" ve örgütlü suçlar ile kadına ve çocuğa yönelik kasten öldürme, cinsel saldırı ve çocuğun cinsel istismarı da kapsam dışı bırakıldı.**
11. Yargı Paketi'nde yer alan trafikte yol kesmek, bir aracı durdurmak veya engelleyene verilecek 3 yıl kadar hapis cezası maddesine verilen önerge kabul edildi. Yürüyüş ve gösteri yapanlar kapsam dışı tutuldu.
TBMM Genel Kurulu'nda kabul edilen 11 Yargı Paketi'nin 27. Maddesi'nde yapılan değişiklikle, "deprem nedeniyle bina veya diğer yapıların yıkılması, çökmesi ya da hasar alması sonucu meydana gelen öldürme suçlarından hüküm giyenler" denetimli serbestlikle erken tahliye hükümlerinden yararlanamayacak.
Deprem suçlularına tahliye imkânı, yargı paketindeki "Covid affı" olarak adlandırılan düzenleme kapsamında doğmuştu.
[Reklam](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/kurumsal/2015/01/000000_advertising_faqs)
6 Şubat depremlerinde yakınlarını kaybedenlerin kurduğu "Adalet Peşinde Aileler Platformu", 11. Yargı Paketi'nin Genel Kurul gündemine gelmesi üzerine Meclis önünde eylemler yaptı, ayrıca iktidar ve muhalefet partilerinin grup başkanlarıyla görüşmeler yaparak, deprem suçlarından yargılananların af kapsamı dışında tutulmasını istedi.
## '2500 deprem davası dosyası'
AKP ve MHP, deprem kaynaklı ölüme sebebiyet verenlerin "Covid affı" düzenlemesi dışında tutulması formülünde uzlaşmıştı.
AKP kaynaklarının verdiği bilgiye göre sadece 6 Şubat 2023'teki Kahramanmaraş merkezli depremleri sonrası açılan 2 bin 500 deprem davası dosyası bulunuyor.

# Meclis'teki kavga ile ilgili AKP ve CHP birbirini suçladı
![AKP ve CHP milletvekilleri arasındaki gerilim yumruklu kavgaya dönüştü.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/1ae8/live/88942860-dec5-11f0-8f45-6b51b3fbca7e.png.webp)
Kaynak, T24
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, AKP ve CHP milletvekilleri arasındaki gerilim yumruklu kavgaya dönüştü.**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer, Londra
  * 22 Aralık 2025
  * Okuma süresi: 5 dk


[_**Gündemi BBC Türkçe'den takip etmek artık WhatsApp'ta da mümkün. Haberlerimizin doğrudan telefonunuza gelmesi için tıklayın.**_](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c87lyvevwz8o)
**2026 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi ile 2024 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi, Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi'nde (TBMM) kavgalı bir oturum sonrası kabul edildi.**
Çıkan kavgada, AKP ile CHP'li bazı milletvekilleri birbirlerini yumrukladı. 
AKP'nin 22 Aralık'taki Merkez Yürütme Kurulu toplantısı sonrası soru üzerine konu hakkında konuşan parti sözcüsü Ömer Çelik, AKP Adıyaman Milletvekili İshak Şan'a "saldıran" CHP'linin cezalandırılmasını istedi.
İshak Şan, kavga sırasında yumruklanmıştı.
"CHP Grup Başkanvekilleri gelip üzüntülerini belirttiler, bunu da önemli buluyoruz" diyen Çelik, sözlerini şöyle sürdürdü: 
"Bu barbarlık ve vandallığı yapan kişiye yönelik ceza sürecinin CHP tarafından başlatılması son derecek kıymetli olacaktır." 
CHP Genel Başkanı Özgür Özel ise aynı gün gerçekleşen Cumhurbaşkanlığı Aday Ofisi Yürütme Kurulu toplantısı sonrası yaptığı açıklamada, isim vermeden AKP Milletvekili Mustafa Varank'a yüklendi.
[Haberin başlığını atlayın ve okumaya devam edin](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cpq43r5qv12o#end-of-recommendations)

Haberin sonu
TBMM'de 80 dakika boyunca polemik yapmadan çözüm önerilerini anlattığını söyleyen Özel, iktidar partisini "yapıcı eleştiriye ve çözüme odaklanan bir yaklaşıma karşın polemik dilini tercih etmekle" suçladı.
"Muhalefet partilerine, bilhassa ana muhalefet partisine [yönelik olarak], nasıl haksız, nasıl düzeysiz ve nasıl polemikle kendilerine yönelik eleştirileri örtmek isteyen bir dil tercih ettiklerini siz gördünüz" dedi.
Özel, isim vermeden Mustafa Varank'ı "milletvekillerini birbirine düşürmekle" suçladı:
"Geçmişte bakanlık görevinde bulunan, şimdi komisyona hapsoldu diye partisiyle sorunları olan birisinin bu parlamentoya yakışmayan ve kendi partisinin yöneticileri tarafından da eleştirilen, en sonunda da milletvekillerini birbirine düşürünce hu

# Turkey arrests 115 IS suspects 'planning New Year's attacks'
13 minutes ago
Share
Save
Sean Seddon
Share
Save
![Getty Images People walking down a street in Istabul](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/e997/live/44d2a2c0-e17a-11f0-b74d-8b6955ff49d6.jpg.webp)Getty Images
Prosecutors in Istanbul say attacks targeting non-Muslims this week were being planned by the group
Turkish authorities say they have disrupted planned attacks on Christmas and New Year's events after arresting more than 100 suspected members of the so-called Islamic State group.
Mass raids were carried out at 124 addresses across Istanbul, the capital's chief prosecutor said, with firearms, ammunition and "organisational documents" seized.
Officials said IS supporters had been actively planning attacks across Turkey this week, particularly against non-Muslims. 
Police detained 115 suspects but efforts are ongoing to trace a further 22, an official statement read.
The prosecutor's office said the suspects were in contact with IS operatives outside Turkey. 
The announcement comes two days after Turkish intelligence agents carried out a raid against the group on the Afghanistan-Pakistan border.
A Turkish national who is alleged to have held a senior role with the IS wing operating in the region was detained and accused of planning attacks against civilians. 
Turkey's security services regularly target people with suspected links to IS.
The country shares a 900km (560 mile) border with Syria, where the group continues to operate in parts of the country.
Syria's president Ahmed al-Sharaa, who has close ties to the Turkish government, has vowed to work with the US and Europe to root out surviving elements of IS. 
The US launched a wave of air strikes against the group's positions across Syria on Friday in response to the killing of three Americans.
Two US soldiers and a civilian interpreter

# Kurtulmuş komisyona eleştirilere yanıt verdi: 'SDG'nin silah bırakmasını komisyon nasıl sağlayacak?'
![Numan Kurtulmuş beyaz bir deri koltukta oturuyor, arkada türk bayrağı var. ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/b837/live/7cc00c80-e0c7-11f0-865d-e31ee148330a.png.webp)
Kaynak, TBMM
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Numan Kurtulmuş - 21 Kasım tarihli komisyon toplantısında**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, Ayşe Sayın
    * Bildirdiği yer, Ankara
  * 24 Aralık 2025
  * Okuma süresi: 4 dk


**Rapor yazım aşamasına gelen Milli Dayanışma Kardeşlik ve Demokrasi Komisyonu'nun görev süresi yapılan 20'inci toplantıda iki ay uzatıldı.**
Kurulan komisyonun görev süresi 31 Aralık'ta sona eriyordu. 
Komisyonun başkanlığını da üstlenen TBMM Başkanı Numan Kurtulmuş, komisyon çalışmalarının Ekim ayında bitirilmesini hedeflediklerini, ancak sürecin uzadığına dikkat çekti.
"Bütün meseleyi çözecek olan komisyon değil. SDG'nin silah bırakmasını komisyon nasıl sağlayacak?" diye konuştu.
[Reklam](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/kurumsal/2015/01/000000_advertising_faqs)
Komisyonun açılışında konuşan Kurtulmuş, siyasi partilerin [raporlarını sunduğunu](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/clydp4y7kyzo) belirttikten sonra raporların [ortaklaştırılması ](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cg4ndgl6g9zo)için çalışma yürüteceklerini söyledi.
Raporların iletilmesi ardından ilk kez basına açık toplanan komisyonda akademisyenler Prof. Dr. Havva Kök Aslan ve Dr. Murat Sevecan, toplantı tutanak analizlerine ilişkin sunum yaptılar.
Sunumda, "sürecin nihai hedefi konusunda belirsizliklerin devam ettiği" vurgulanırken, "Güvenlik-özgürlük dengesi konusunda aktörler arası yaklaşımlar belirgin biçimde ayrışıyor" görüşüne yer verdi.

# 'Barış Boyun suç örgütü' davasında örgüt yöneticilerine 12'şer kez müebbet
![İtalya'da düzenlenen operasyona katılan farklı ülkelerin polisleri ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/4bf0/live/65c35e80-e0cf-11f0-aae2-2191c0e48a3b.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Anadolu via Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Barış Boyun ve 16 şüpheli, 22 Mayıs 2024'te İtalya'da düzenlenen operasyonla yakalanmıştı.**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer, İstanbul 
  * 24 Aralık 2025
  * Okuma süresi: 4 dk


**Barış Boyun'un liderliğini yaptığı belirtilen, 362 sanıklı organize suç örgütü davasında karar açıklandı.**
İstanbul 16. Ağır Ceza Mahkemesi'nde görülen davanın karar duruşmasında, örgüt yöneticilerinden Bahadır Akdağ ve Zafer Boyun'a 12'şer kez müebbet hapis cezası verildi. 
258 sanık ise bir ila 700 yıl arasında çeşitli hapis cezalarına çarptırıldı.
Silivri'deki Marmara Kapalı Ceza İnfaz Kurumu'nun karşısındaki salonda yapılan duruşma sırasında sanıklar ile jandarma arasında gerginlik yaşandı.
Jandarma biber gazıyla olaylara müdahale etti.
Bakırköy Cumhuriyet Başsavcılığı, konuyla ilgili soruşturma başlatıldığını duyurdu.
Dava konusu örgüt, "yeni nesil mafya" olarak tanımlanan yapıların en önemlilerinden biri olarak görülüyor.
Kamuoyunda Daltonlar olarak bilinen ve bir dönem Barış Boyun ile birlikte hareket edip sonra bağımsız hareket etmeye başlayan, Beratcan Gökdemir'in liderliğini yaptığı öne sürülen suç örgütüyle ilgili ayrı bir dava ise devam ediyor.
  * [Türkiye ve İtalya'nın gündemindeki 'Barış Boyun suç örgütü' hakkında neler biliniyor?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c04n5r9wey0o)


## 62 sanığa beraat
305 sanık için açılan davada birleşen dosyalarla birlikte toplam 362 sanık yargılandı.
Mahkeme heyeti, örgüt lideri Barış Boyun ile örgüt yöneticileri Beratcan Gökdemir, Tolga Gültepe ve Bayram Demir'in de aralarında bulunduğu 34 sanığın henüz yakalanmadıkları için dosyalarının ayrılmasına karar verdi.
Heyet, Bahadır Akdağ ve Zafer Boyun'a aralarında kasten adam öldürmenin de bulunduğu çeşitli suçlardan 12'şer kez müebbet hapis cezası verdi.
  * [İstanbul'da 'yeni nesil mafya': Mahallelerde neler yaşanıyor?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c70j4edg1e2o)


258 sanık hakkında ise bir ila 700 yıl arasında çeşitli hapis cezaları verildi.
Davada sanık olarak yer alan rap şarkıcılarından "Heijan" lakaplı Doğan Tarda ve "Muti" lakaplı Muhammet Nedim Doğan, üzerlerine atılı suçlardan beraat etti.
"CAC" lakaplı Şahan Terzioğlu ile "BIG" lakaplı Samet Işık ise "suç örgütüne üye olmak" ve "suç işlemeye tahrik" suçlarından dörder yıl yedişer ay hapis cezası aldı.
Heyet, toplamda 62 sanığın beraatına karar verdi.
15 sanığın ise tutuklanmasını kararlaştırdı.
## Örgüt hakkında neler biliniyor?
İstanbul Cumhuriyet Başsavcılığı tarafından hazırlanan iddianame Eylül 2024'te kabul edildi.
BBC Türkçe'nin incelediği iddianamede, "Boyun'un çıkar amaçlı silahlı suç örgütü kurduğu, bu örgütün elebaşı olduğu, örgüt kurma suçundan hakkında yakalama kararı bulunduğu ve örgüt yöneticilerine suç işleme kastıyla talimatlar verdiği" öne sürülüyor.
578 sayfalık iddianamede geleneksel suç örgütlerinden birçok açıdan farklılık gösteren gruba yönelik dikkat çekici tespitler yer alıyor.
![Silivri'deki Marmara Cezaevi'nde görev yapan bir jandarma ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/1363/live/f2747dc0-e0d2-11f0-94d6-33dfd766fcaa.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, OZAN KOSE/AFP/Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Dava Silivri'deki Marmara Cezaevi'nde görüldü. (Arşiv fotoğrafı)
İddianameye göre, 1984 doğumlu Boyun'un önderliğindeki grup, başlangıçta mahalle düzeyinde bir örgütlenmeye sahipti ve İstanbul'un Beyoğlu ilçesindeki Hasköy semtinde faaliyet gösteriyordu.
Grup, bölgede hakimiyet sağlamak için kendisine hasım gördüğü gruplarla rant kavgasına girdi.
  * [Daltonlar: 'Yeni nesil' bir suç örgütünün anatomisi](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cq50z4v79xjo)


Örgütün, küçük bir yapılanmayken büyük gruplara taşeronluk ve tetikçilik yaparak büyüdüğü ve büyük bir silahlı suç örgütüne dönüştüğü iddia ediliyor.
Bu süreçte grubun, uluslararası mafya grupları arasında da taşeronluk yaptığı belirtiliyor.
Örneğin iddianamede Barış Boyun'un Avrupa'da uyuşturucu ticareti yapan bir Sırp suç örgütü ile işbirliği yaptığı belirtiliyor. 
![Boyun'un faaliyetlerine ilk olarak İstanbul'un Beyoğlu ilçesinde başladığı aktarılıyor. ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/7258/live/14d3ce50-e0d0-11f0-b67b-690eb873de1b.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Oleksii Chumachenko/SOPA Images/LightRocket/Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Boyun'un faaliyetlerine ilk olarak İstanbul'un Beyoğlu ilçesinde başladığı aktarılıyor. 
İddianamede, grubun üyelerini 15-20'li yaşlardaki kişilerden seçtikleri aktarılıyor. 
Örgüte sokulan bu çocuk ve gençlerin ülke içerisinde "kamikaze dronu" gibi kullanıldığı tespiti yapılıyor.

Mali, Burkina Faso and Niger have announced the launch of a [joint military battalion](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/24/burkina-faso-leader-vows-sahel-alliance-crackdown-on-armed-groups) aimed at fighting armed groups across the Sahel, one of Africa’s poorest and most volatile regions.
The initiative was announced at the end of the two-day Alliance of Sahel States (AES) summit in the Malian capital, Bamako, as the three countries struggle to improve the security situation amid rising attacks from separatist groups as well as armed groups linked to al-Qaeda and ISIL (ISIS).

end of list
This was the group’s second summit since its formation in 2023.
Here is what you need to know about the summit and whether the joint battalion will help improve the security situation in the three Sahel countries.
## What was agreed on?
They agreed to launch a joint battalion, which is expected to comprise an estimated 5,000 soldiers from the three countries, with a mandate centred on counterterrorism and border security.
Burkina Faso leader Ibrahim Traore, who was named the head of the alliance, announced “large-scale” joint operations against armed groups in the coming days.
Moreover, the three leaders also jointly launched the AES Television, described in official communications as an instrument to counter disinformation and promote the region’s narrative.
A Burkinabe presidency statement said the leaders would review implementation reports, adopt decisions to consolidate achievements and address major challenges facing the bloc.
[General Omar Tchiani](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/27/who-is-omar-tchiani-the-suspected-brain-behind-niger-coup), leader of Niger’s military government, said the AES had “put an end to all occupation forces in our countries”. “No country or interest group will decide for our countries any more,” he said.
Advertisement 
## Has the reliance on Russian forces improved the security situation?
The military leaders of the three nations in recent years kicked out longtime security partners [France](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/12/31/au-revoir-sahel-did-2023-crush-frances-influence-in-africa) and the United States. Thousands of French soldiers were stationed in several African nations, including the three Sahel countries, while Niger hosted nearly 1,000 US soldiers and was the site of the largest drone base in Africa. US forces withdrew from Niger last year.
notification-importantGet instant alerts and updates based on your interests. Be the first to know when big stories happen.
Yes, keep me updated
After snapping ties with their Western partners, the military leaders from the Sahel countries turned to [Russia](https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2025/9/25/the-us-and-russias-proxy-war-is-bleeding-africa) amid an increasingly vulnerable security situation.
Bamako is now collaborating with Russian forces, with about 1,500 personnel from the Wagner mercenary group initially, and since June, roughly 1,000 fighters from the Kremlin-controlled paramilitary group Africa Corps.
Russian soldiers are also present, though in smaller numbers, in Burkina Faso and Niger.
On the apparent contradiction of allowing [Russian mercenaries](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/6/is-mali-about-to-fall-to-al-qaeda-affiliate-jnim) to operate on their soil while claiming independence from foreign influence, analyst Ulf Laessing says it is a message from the military-run nations to the West with whom they would like to “work less”.

The Libyan army’s chief of staff, Mohammed Ali Ahmed al-Haddad, has died in a plane crash near the Turkish capital, Ankara, shortly after the private jet he was travelling in took off from an airport there.
The crash on Tuesday killed everyone on board.

The other victims were four high-ranking Libyan military officials as well as three crew members.
Turkish officials told Al Jazeera that initial investigations have ruled out sabotage and instead point to a technical failure as the cause of the crash.
On Wednesday, Turkish Interior Minister Ali Yerlikaya said the black box from the jet had been recovered. “The examination and evaluation processes of these devices have been initiated by the relevant authorities,” he told reporters at the site in Haymana district.

The Libyan army’s Chief of Staff, Lieutenant General Mohammed Ali Ahmed al-Haddad, [has been killed in a plane crash](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/23/signal-lost-with-jet-carrying-libyan-army-chief-over-ankara-turkiye-says) in Turkiye while returning from an official visit to Ankara.
Turkish officials said the private aircraft, which was heading back to Tripoli on Tuesday, requested an emergency landing due to an electrical failure just minutes after takeoff, but then lost contact.

# Syrian army, Kurdish-led SDF agree to stop deadly fighting in Aleppo
_At least two people killed in clashes in northern city of Aleppo during Turkish FM Fidan’s visit to Syria._
![Syria](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/AP25356658125609-1766469560.jpg?resize=770%2C513&quality=80)
Flames rising from a building after clashes broke out between Syrian army and Kurdish-led SDF in neighborhoods of the northern Syrian city of Aleppo [SANA via AP]
By News Agencies and Reuters
Published On 23 Dec 202523 Dec 2025
Click here to share on social media
share2
Share
Save
Syrian government forces and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces have agreed to stop fighting in the northern city of Aleppo, after a [wave of attacks](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/12/22/clashes-between-syrian-army-kurdish-led-sdf-break-out-in-aleppo) left at least two civilians dead and several injured.
On Monday, Syria’s state news agency cited the Ministry of Defence as saying the army’s general command issued an order to stop targeting the SDF’s fighters after the deadly clashes erupted during a visit by Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan.

Clashes have been reported between the Syrian army and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) in Aleppo amid high-level talks on how to integrate the SDF into Syria’s state institutions.
Syria’s state-run SANA news agency reported on Monday that the SDF launched attacks against Syrian security force positions near the Sheihan and Lairmoun roundabouts in Aleppo.
“The Gaziantep-Aleppo road has been closed from the direction of the Lairmoun and Sheihan roundabouts as SDF targets the road,” the news agency said.
The Syrian Civil Defence said two of its rescuers were injured after the vehicle they were travelling in was fired upon by the SDF.
“The vehicle clearly displayed the Civil Defense insignia,” the rescue agency said, adding that the two wounded rescuers were taken to hospital.

Turkey expects the second phase of a Gaza ceasefire deal to begin early in 2026, Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said on Monday, following talks with US, Qatari and Egyptian officials in Miami over the weekend.
Speaking at a press conference in Damascus, Fidan said the discussions focused on obstacles to advancing the deal to its next phase, adding that the priority was for Gaza’s governance to be taken over by a Palestinian-led group.

# Greek among victims of jet crash in Turkey carrying Libyan army chief
![Greek among victims of jet crash in Turkey carrying Libyan army chief](https://www.ekathimerini.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/X2J2VDU5CFMC5A447ZOLX3HJTE-960x600.jpg?v=1766564682)
Turkish security forces and search and rescue teams work around the crash site of a jet carrying Libya's army chief of staff Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad near Kesikkavak village, Turkey, December 24, 2025. [Cagla Gurdogan/Reuters]
[ Manolis Kostidis ](https://www.ekathimerini.com/author/manolis-kostidis/ "View all posts by Manolis Kostidis")
December 24, 2025 24.12.2025 • 10:24
A Greek woman is among the eight dead passengers of the private Dassault Falcon 50 jet carrying the Libyan army’s chief of staff, Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad, [that crashed Tuesday shortly after take off in Turkey](https://www.ekathimerini.com/politics/foreign-policy/1290774/libyan-armys-chief-dies-in-plane-crash-in-turkey/).
According to Turkish media, the Greek citizen was one of the plane’s crew members.
The Greek embassy in Ankara has not been informed that a Greek citizen was among the dead.
The Tripoli-bound aircraft had taken off from Ankara Esenboga Airport at 7.17 p.m. Greek time.
The private jet reported an electrical failure and requested an emergency landing shortly before crashing near Ankara, Turkey’s head of communications has said.

# US to Turkey: Don’t escalate
![US to Turkey: Don’t escalate](https://www.ekathimerini.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/AP-F16-960x600.jpg)
[ Lena Argiri ](https://www.ekathimerini.com/author/lena-argiri/ "View all posts by Lena Argiri")
December 24, 2025 24.12.2025 • 19:38
The US administration has lately taken to prodding Turkey – in a friendly manner and with no finger-wagging, diplomatic sources tell Kathimerini, to avoid tensions with its neighbors.
In light of this, Greek diplomats wonder if Friday’s mock-combat engagement of a Greek and a Turkish F-16 fighter, the first since February 2, 2023, was a one-off, or a sign that Turkey is ignoring the message.
Turkey wants to buy F-35 planes from the United States and cannot afford to ignore it.
It is even prepared to ditch the Russian S-400 anti-aircraft missiles it bought by returning them, something it previously excluded as a humiliation and practically impossible.

Monday’s tripartite meeting between the leaders of Greece, Cyprus and Israel in Jerusalem has drawn strong reactions in Turkey, with sections of the Turkish media portraying the talks as an “alliance of evil” and an “alliance against Turkey.”
Turkish outlets have broadly framed the meeting as a move to strengthen coordination among Athens, Nicosia and Jerusalem in response to Ankara’s regional influence.
Pro-government newspapers such as Hurriyet and Millet reported that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, whom they described as concerned about Turkey’s growing role in the eastern Mediterranean, met in Jerusalem with Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis and Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides.
Greece and Cyprus, the reports noted, are seeking to deepen military cooperation with Israel and to secure involvement in a planned international force in Gaza.
At Monday’s tripartite, Mitsotakis, Christodoulides and Netanyahu agreed to deepen security cooperation.

# Libyan army’s chief dies in plane crash in Turkey
![Libyan army’s chief dies in plane crash in Turkey](https://www.ekathimerini.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/HRC3KCDUVNIKFBE5GLFGH43EBQ-1-960x600.jpg)
Libyan Chief of Staff Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad (right) meets with his Turkish counterpart Selcuk Bayraktaroglu in Ankara, Turkey, December 23, 2025. [Turkish Defense Ministry/Handout via Reuters]
Tuvan Gumrukcu and Ahmed Elumami
December 24, 2025 24.12.2025 • 08:29
The Libyan army’s chief of staff, Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad, died in a plane crash on Tuesday after leaving Turkey’s capital Ankara, the prime minister of Libya’s internationally recognized government said, adding that four others were on the jet as well.
“This followed a tragic and painful incident while they were returning from an official trip from the Turkish city of Ankara. This grave loss is a great loss for the nation, for the military institution, and for all the people,” Libyan Prime Minister Abdulhamid Dbeibah said in a statement.
He said the commander of Libya’s ground forces, the director of its military manufacturing authority, an adviser to the chief of staff, and a photographer from the chief of staff’s office were also on the aircraft.
The private jet carrying the Libyan chief of staff reported an electrical failure and requested an emergency landing shortly before crashing near Ankara, Turkey’s head of communications has said.

# Signal lost with jet believed to be carrying Libyan army chief over Ankara, media says
![Signal lost with jet believed to be carrying Libyan army chief over Ankara, media says](https://www.ekathimerini.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/shutterstock_22641196551-960x600.jpg)
[Shutterstock]
[ Newsroom ](https://www.ekathimerini.com/author/newsroom/ "View all posts by Newsroom")
December 23, 2025 23.12.2025 • 21:58
Radio contact was lost with a private jet believed to be carrying Libya’s army chief of staff, Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad, over the Turkish capital Ankara, broadcaster NTV reported on Tuesday, as flight tracking data showed flights being diverted away from Ankara’s Esenboga airport.
Turkey’s defense ministry had announced the Libyan chief of staff’s visit to Ankara earlier this week, saying he had met his Turkish counterpart and other military commanders. [Reuters]

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, centre, Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides, left, and Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis hold a joint press conference after a trilateral meeting at the Citadel of David Hotel in Jerusalem, on December 22, 2025. [Abir Sultan/Pool Photo via AP]

The leaders of Greece, Israel and Cyprus used a scheduled trilateral summit in Jerusalem to reaffirm political cooperation and send a pointed message to regional rivals, particularly Turkey, which views the format as undermining its interests in the Eastern Mediterranean.
Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides said the partnership would be maintained at the highest level, stressing resilience amid heightened regional tensions. Netanyahu referred to Turkey, saying the trilateral grouping “does not seek conflict with anyone but rather seeks stability.”

![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/crash-1-1170x600.jpg)
**A private jet carrying a high-level Libyan military delegation, including the Chief of Staff of the Libyan Armed Forces, General Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad, crashed near Ankara on Tuesday evening, Turkish authorities said.**
According to Turkey’s Interior Ministry, contact was lost with the aircraft, a Falcon 50 business jet operated by Falcon Air, shortly after it departed from Ankara’s Esenboğa Airport bound for Tripoli. The jet took off at 8:10 p.m., and communication was lost at 8:52 p.m.
Interior Minister Ali Yerlikaya said an emergency landing request was received from the aircraft near the Haymana area, south of Ankara, but contact could not be re-established. Five people were on board, including General Al-Haddad.
Flight tracking data showed that Ankara’s airspace was temporarily closed, with flights diverted away from the airport following the incident.
Earlier on Tuesday, Turkish Defence Minister Yaşar Güler had met with General Al-Haddad, while Turkey’s Chief of the General Staff, General Selçuk Bayraktaroğlu, officially welcomed him at General Staff headquarters during his visit to Turkey as an official guest.
Turkish daily _Hurriyet_ reported that a loud noise was heard near the Haymana area, and an investigation into the incident has already begun.
Turkey’s Defence Ministry had earlier announced this week the visit of Libya’s Chief of Staff to the country.
Authorities said the public would be informed of further developments as more information becomes available.

**NEWSROOM | GREEKCITYTIMES.GR**  
23/12/2025 21:16 | UPDATED: 23/12/2025 21:33
A private Falcon 50 aircraft disappeared from radar screens after taking off from Ankara’s Esenboğa Airport, Turkish media report.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/1000218900-1024x512.webp)**Dassault Falcon 50 aircraft**  
 _(Stock image / Shutterstock)_
Esenboğa Airport was temporarily closed, and flights were diverted to Kayseri Airport.
The Falcon 50 was en route to Tripoli, and it is believed that Libya’s Chief of General Staff was on board. 0 “LARGE” 1 “LARGE” 2 “LARGE”
**Statement from the Turkish Minister of Interior**
Turkish Interior Minister Ali Yerlikaya posted on X: “Contact was lost at 8:52 p.m. tonight with a Falcon 50 business jet, registration number 9H-DFJ, which took off from Ankara’s Esenboğa Airport at 8:10 p.m. bound for Tripoli. The aircraft requested an emergency landing near Haymana. However, subsequent contact with the aircraft was not possible. There are five passengers on board, including Libya’s Chief of the Armed Forces, General Mohammed Ali Ahmed Al-Haddad. The public will be informed of further developments.”
**Note:** International reports confirm the loss of contact shortly after takeoff on December 23, 2025. Some Turkish sources later reported that the jet crashed near Ankara following a technical issue, but details on casualties remain unconfirmed as of the article’s publication time.

![Turkish media Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/1-22.jpg)
**Ankara** – Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan launched a scathing verbal attack on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday, dismissing recent statements from a high-profile trilateral summit in Jerusalem as meaningless noise.
The remarks came during an address to provincial leaders of his ruling Justice and Development Party (AKP), amid ongoing tensions sparked by the December 22 meeting between Netanyahu, Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis, and Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/leaders-of-Cyprus-Greece-and-Israel-1024x576.jpg)
Erdogan expressed irritation over a recent maritime delimitation agreement between the Republic of Cyprus and Lebanon, before directing his sharpest criticism at Netanyahu.
“Whether in the eastern Mediterranean, the Aegean, or anywhere else, we do not usurp anyone’s rights, nor do we allow anyone to usurp our rights. We will not allow the rights and interests of the Turkish Cypriots to be violated,” Erdogan said, referring to the Turkish-occupied northern part of Cyprus.
“They can conclude agreements, place signatures and send various messages… None of this binds us or changes our policy,” he continued. “The words of those who have stained their hands with the blood of our Palestinian brothers are no different, in our eyes, from the clatter of tin cans… We did not fall for their game, and we won’t fall for it. We did not succumb to provocations.”
Reaffirming Turkey’s stance on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, Erdogan pledged increased aid to Gaza: “During these blessed three months, we will increase our aid to Palestine. As Turkey, we will not back down, we will not remain silent, we will not forget, and we will never leave Gaza alone… Everyone should know that, as in the past, we are in favor of peace and tranquility today. But that does not mean that we consent to injustice or that we remain silent in the face of oppression. Never.”
The comments appear to be a direct response to Netanyahu’s remarks at the Jerusalem summit, where – without naming Turkey or Erdogan – the Israeli leader warned: “To those who dream of establishing empires and dominating our lands, I say: forget it. That will not happen. Don’t even think about it… We are committed and able to defend ourselves, and this cooperation enhances our capabilities.
The trilateral meeting focused on deepening cooperation in energy, security, defense, and other areas, underscoring the strategic alliance among the three democracies in the Eastern Mediterranean amid regional rivalries. 5 “LARGE”
Tensions between Turkey and the Israel-Greece-Cyprus axis have persisted over maritime boundaries, energy resources, and the Cyprus division, with Ankara viewing the partnership as a challenge to its interests in the region.

![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Foto_-1170x600.jpg)
**Turkish authorities have taken the president of Fenerbahce into custody in Istanbul on December 24 as part of a drug investigation that involves prominent figures from the entertainment and media sectors, according to state media.**
Sadettin Saran, who holds dual Turkish and United States citizenship, was detained just hours after forensic tests detected traces of narcotics in his hair samples, state broadcaster TRT reported.
Prosecutors had summoned him last week to give testimony, after which officials transferred him to a forensic unit to provide hair and blood samples.
Since the beginning of December, more than a dozen people have been placed under detention as part of the investigation being conducted under the supervision of the Istanbul Prosecutor’s Office.
Those detained include television presenters, journalists, singers, actors and social media personalities. They face charges ranging from the production and trafficking of drugs to facilitating prostitution. Many of them have undergone blood and hair tests for drug use.
Saran, who was born in Denver and was elected president of Fenerbahce in September, was questioned last week on suspicion of supplying and facilitating the use of narcotic substances.

![Turkey, israel turkey mitsotakis netanyahu erdogan](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/0-5.jpg)
#### Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held**[a summit in East Jerusalem](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/israel-greece-cyprus-deepen-military-security-ties-jerusalem-summit-2025/?amp)** on Monday with Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis and President of the Republic of Cyprus Nikos Christodoulides.
**[Turkish media](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/turkish-media-israel-sand-castle/)** cited Israeli analysts who characterized the meeting between the leaders of Greece, Israel, and Cyprus as driven by shared concerns about Turkey.
According to **[Hürriyet](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/09/08/turkish-media-vetoes-turkey-safe/)** , the cooperation among the three countries, which began with energy cooperation about 15 years ago, is moving toward a military alliance. The deepening of relations between Greece, Cyprus, and **[Israel](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/22/turkey-key-issue-israel-greece-cyprus-trilateral-summit-2025/)** in recent months has gone beyond a series of closed-door meetings.
Unlike their Greek counterparts, the Turkish outlet said, the Israeli media characterized the cooperation among the three countries as an “alliance,” announcing that the **[Mitsotakis](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/israel-greece-cyprus-trilateral-summit-2025-jerusalem/)** and Christodoulides governments were building a comprehensive partnership with Netanyahu in the Eastern Mediterranean. While Greece and Cyprus are presented as “traditional adversaries” of **[Turkey](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/turkey-plan-greece-india-israel/)** , it is noted that the “triple alliance” is based on the pursuit of forming a common front against Turkey.
Israeli media reported that the alliance emerged amid tensions between Israel and Ankara and that “natural gas discoveries in the Israeli open sea, the anti-Zionist policies of President **[Recep Tayyip Erdoga](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/21/erdogan-turkey-has-no-territorial-ambitions-as-new-submarine-enters-service-under-blue-homeland-doctrine/?amp)** n, and Turkey’s sensitivity to the humanitarian crisis in **[Gaza](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/greek-prime-minister-meets-palestinian-authority-president-abbas/)** have led Tel Aviv to seek new allies in the region.”
The Star newspaper adopted aggressive language, personally targeting **[Netanyahu](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/18/israel-egypt-112-billion-gas-deal-netanyahu-2025/)** , whom it described as a “leader of genocide,” while presenting his statement that “we do not seek conflict with Turkey” as a sign of retreat and weakness.
Correspondingly, A Haber spoke of a “dirty alliance in the Mediterranean”, accusing Greece, Cyprus and Israel of openly targeting Turkey. **[Haber7](https://greekcitytimes.com/2023/01/31/cihat-yayci-turkey-nato-israel/)** went even further, speaking of Netanyahu’s “terrible threat” to Ankara, citing his phrase that “he hopes the alliance will not be tested”.
The nationalist **[Aydınlık](https://greekcitytimes.com/2023/06/22/turkish-newspaper-aydinlik-goats/)** included the Tripartite in the “Blue Homeland” narrative, describing it as a Greek Cypriot and Israeli alliance that seeks to thwart Turkish plans in the Eastern Mediterranean. Artı Gerçek moved on the same wavelength, highlighting the decision to strengthen military and defense cooperation between the three countries.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue_Homeland-1024x802.jpg)Turkey’s envisioned Blue Homeland that aims to steal Greek and Cypriot maritime space.
Of particular interest was Turkey’s coverage, which linked the summit to broader geopolitical messages towards Ankara, speaking of “blocking accounts in **[Tel Aviv](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/11/13/kevin-spacey-chooses-israel-over-greece-boycott/)** ” and an indirect Turkish response to the developments.
Parallel contacts and statements regarding the Palestinian issue were also included in the Turkish publicity. Haber7 featured statements by Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, who called for pressure to freeze Israeli settlements in the West Bank and expressed support for Greece for a possible role in Gaza, presenting **[Athens](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/19/greek-police-smash-athens-drug-ring-run-by-foreign-nationals/)** as a player with a growing regional presence.
Overall, the Turkish media has treated the Tripartite not as a scheme for regional stability, but as a coordinated move to geopolitically encircle Turkey. The intensity of the rhetoric reflects Ankara’s discomfort with the institutional deepening of the Greece-Cyprus-Israeli cooperation and the fact that this scheme is gaining increasing international legitimacy, with support from the US and connections to broader initiatives in the **[Eastern Mediterranean](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/turkish-warplan

# Turkey's bombshell plan targets Greece, India and Israel
_by_ [Paul Antonopoulos](https://greekcitytimes.com/authors/paul-antonopoulos/ "Posts by Paul Antonopoulos")
![Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan Turkey](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/turkey-plan-greece-india-israel/)
[0](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/23/turkey-plan-greece-india-israel/)
#### Amid a climate of intense geopolitical turmoil, Turkish President **[Recep Tayyip Erdoğan](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/21/erdogan-turkey-has-no-territorial-ambitions-as-new-submarine-enters-service-under-blue-homeland-doctrine/?amp)** appears, according to international analyses, to be advancing a grandiose plan to upset the balance of power from the Eastern Mediterranean to the Indian Ocean. Turkey's recent strategic partnership with **[Pakistan](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/08/08/the-turkey-libya-pakistan-axis-threatens-greek-interests/)** is not presented simply as an economic agreement for energy research, but as a development with potentially serious geopolitical implications.
The cooperation with Islamabad reportedly opens to Ankara a "gateway" to operational presence in the Indian Ocean for the first time, which - as analysts argue - could even affect vital sea lanes related to **[Israel](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/18/israel-egypt-112-billion-gas-deal-netanyahu-2025/)** and other countries.
At the same time, concerns are being raised about Turkey's possible access to Pakistani nuclear networks, creating scenarios of a possible "nuclear shield" if there is financial support from Ankara to Islamabad.
At the same time, the strengthening of technological cooperation, especially in the field of **[drones](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/10/23/greek-drones-production-hellenic-army/)** , is presented by experts as another link in a chain that forms a new, worrying power bloc. Turkey is reportedly attempting to acquire a key role not only in the Mediterranean but also in Central and South Asia, affecting sensitive geopolitical balances – with **[India](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/05/02/maj-gen-bakshi-india-turkey/)** , Greece, **[Cyprus](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/06/17/from-nicosia-to-imec-modis-strategic-visit-to-cyprus-and-the-new-chapter-in-india-and-greece-relations/)** , and Israel at the center of concerns.
At the same time, international observers point out that a possible expansion of Turkey's military and political footprint, combined with its leading presence in Turkish-speaking and Muslim countries, could give Ankara a role of power reminiscent of other eras in its **[history](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/21/restoration-works-commence-for-ancient-theatre-of-calydon/)**.
Many countries are closely monitoring these developments. According to analysts, Israel, Greece, **[Egypt](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/17/alexander-the-great-returns-to-egypt-in-exhibition/)** , and even **[Russia](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/22/russian-general-fanil-sarvarov-killed-moscow-car-bomb-2025/)** do not want a Turkey with over-strengthened geopolitical influence.
It is not excluded that there will be significant developments in the near future, as the issue of a "Greater Turkey" seems to be a crucial matter not only for the region but also for the West, especially **[the European Union](https://greekcitytimes.com/2025/12/21/greeces-new-id-cards-mandatory-for-eu-travel-from-august-2026/?amp)**.

![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/school-2.jpg)
**Turkish education authorities have approved the temporary relocation of the Patriarchal Great School of the Nation to a former community primary school in Tatavla, enabling seismic reinforcement works on its historic building in Istanbul’s Phanar district amid concerns over a possible major earthquake.**
The school will move for a three-year period to the building of the former community Greek primary school of the Tatavla Community, which no longer operates due to a lack of pupils. The agreement was reached between the school’s administration, led by headmaster Dimitris Zotos, and the Ecclesiastical Committee of the Tatavla Community, chaired by Giorgos Theodoridis.
Representatives of the community board and the school’s director met with Ecumenical Patriarch Bartholomew, who congratulated them on the agreement and gave his blessing for the temporary housing arrangement.
On September 19, the Istanbul Directorate of Education informed the school of the need to reinforce the building’s structural stability following assessments by specialist scientists. The decision предусматривает structural strengthening and repair works that must be carried out without the presence of students, teachers, or staff inside the building, for safety reasons.
The striking red-brick building of the Patriarchal Great School of the Nation, overlooking the Golden Horn, was constructed between 1881 and 1883 on land that once belonged to Dimitrios Kantemir, based on designs by architect Konstantinos Dimadis.
It is the oldest Greek educational institution still in continuous operation, with an uninterrupted history of 571 years. The school was founded in 1454, shortly after the Fall of Constantinople, by Patriarch Gennadios Scholarios, as a continuation of the Ecumenical Patriarchal School established in the 4th century by Emperor Constantine the Great.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/school-2.jpg)

**Maria Pappa: A Double Stroke of Fate**
Maria Pappa, a 42-year-old Greek flight attendant with a lifelong passion for aviation, tragically lost her life in the crash of a Falcon 50 private jet in Turkey on December 23, 2025.
The aircraft, operated by Malta-based Harmony Jets, was carrying Libya’s Chief of General Staff, Lieutenant General Mohammed Ali Ahmed al-Haddad, and several senior military officials back to Tripoli after official talks in Ankara. Shortly after takeoff from Esenboğa Airport, the crew reported an electrical failure and requested an emergency landing. Contact was lost soon after, and the jet crashed in the Haymana region south of Ankara, killing all eight people on board.
For nearly 20 years, Maria had pursued her childhood dream of working in the skies. Colleagues and friends described her as an outstanding professional—sociable, warm, and always ready to help. She frequently shared glimpses of her travels and aerial life on social media, radiating enthusiasm and positivity.
Recently, Maria had transitioned to smaller charter aircraft like the Falcon 50, seeking a new chapter with Harmony Jets. Tragically, this new beginning ended in disaster.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/nb1390-2025-12-24T201609.569-1024x651.webp)
Her death comes as a devastating blow, compounded by personal grief: just months earlier, she lost her mother to cancer. Friends say her demanding work provided solace and strength during that difficult time. One close friend shared, “I still can’t believe it. I keep thinking I’ll see her over the holidays.”
Maria was in a long-term relationship of nearly 20 years and often traveled with her partner. She was unmarried, hardworking, kind, and known for handling challenging situations with grace and professionalism across various airlines.
![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/maria-pappa1.jpg)
Her family awaits procedures for body identification and repatriation, while colleagues express profound shock and sorrow. As one former coworker reflected, “She was a wonderful person who thrived in tough conditions—until the unthinkable happened.”
The crash has prompted investigations, with flight recorders recovered for analysis. Libya has declared national mourning, and the loss reverberates deeply among those who knew Maria.

# U.S. Leaning Toward Setting Up Palestinian-run Regime for Gaza Before International Forces
The Trump administration sees establishing Palestinian rule as critical to stability in the Gaza Strip. Qatar is pushing for such a mechanism to be announced even before Netanyahu visits Florida next week, believing thousands of PA police officers must be deployed immediately to salvage the cease-fire

This time, the opportunity is not only through separate tracks involving Israel-Lebanon, and Israel-Syria, but also as a trilateral arena broadening the range of possibilities. Yet, recent signals from Jerusalem suggest a tilt toward escalation, raising concern that this opportunity may be squandered.

# Libyan army chief killed in jet crash after takeoff from Ankara, Libyan PM confirms
## Flights were diverted away from Ankara Airport following the report, according to publicly available flight-tracking data.
![Private jets are seen on the tarmac at Esenboga Airport, in Ankara, Turkey August 1, 2024](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/698907)Private jets are seen on the tarmac at Esenboga Airport, in Ankara, Turkey August 1, 2024(photo credit: REUTERS/Tunahan Turhan)By[MIRIAM SELA-EITAM](https://www.jpost.com/Author/miriam-sela-eitam), REUTERSDECEMBER 23, 2025 21:10Updated: DECEMBER 23, 2025 23:47
[Libyan](https://www.jpost.com/international/article-881026) Prime Minister Abdulhamid al-Dbeibeh confirmed the death of Libya's Chief of Staff Mohammed Al-Haddad on Tuesday night after a jet carrying him and four others crashed after taking off from Ankara en route to Tripoli.
"This followed a tragic and painful incident while they were returning from an official trip from the Turkish city of Ankara. This grave loss is a great loss for the nation, for the military institution, and for all the people," Libyan Prime Minister Abdulhamid Dbeibah said in a statement.
Libyan Chief of Ground Forces Al-Fitouri Gharibil, Libyan Military Manufacturing Authority Head Mahmoud Al-Qatawi, Libyan Military Advisor Mohammed Al-Asawi, and their companion Mohammed Al-Mahjoub were also confirmed to have died in the crash. 
Authorities have reached the wreckage of the jet, Turkish Interior Minister Ali Yerlikaya said after the confirmation.
Earlier on Tuesday, Yerlikaya said the radio signal from the jet was lost after takeoff. 
Yerlikaya said on X/Twitter that the jet had taken off at 8:10 p.m. and radio contact was lost at 8:52 p.m. He said the flight had requested an emergency landing over the Haymana district of [Ankara,](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-881124) but no further contact was established.
He added that the Dassault Falcon 50-type jet had made a request for an emergency landing while over Haymana, but that no contact was established.

# Turkey detains 115 suspected Islamic State members believed planning Christmas and New Year attacks
## Istanbul Police obtained information that Islamic State members had planned attacks in Turkey during Christmas and New Year celebrations, the prosecutor's office posted on X.
![A member loyal to the Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant \(ISIL\) waves an ISIL flag in Raqqa June 29, 2014](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/677977)A member loyal to the Islamic State in Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) waves an ISIL flag in Raqqa June 29, 2014(photo credit: REUTERS/STRINGER)ByREUTERSDECEMBER 25, 2025 09:22
[Turkish](https://www.jpost.com/tags/turkey) authorities have detained 115 suspected Islamic State members they said were planning to carry out attacks on Christmas and New Year celebrations in the country, the Istanbul chief prosecutor's office said on Thursday.
Istanbul Police obtained information that Islamic State members had planned attacks in Turkey, against non-Muslims in particular, during Christmas and New Year celebrations, the prosecutor's office posted on X.
The police raided 124 locations in Istanbul and captured 115 of the 137 suspects they were seeking, the statement said. Several pistols and ammunition were seized, it said.
![Kashmiri demonstrators hold up a flag of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant \(ISIL\) during a demonstration against Israeli military operations in Gaza, in downtown Srinagar on July 18, 2014. \(credit: TAUSEEF MUSTAFA/AFP via Getty Images\)](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/698051)Kashmiri demonstrators hold up a flag of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) during a demonstration against Israeli military operations in Gaza, in downtown Srinagar on July 18, 2014. (credit: TAUSEEF MUSTAFA/AFP via Getty Images)
### **Attacks in Istanbul and Bondi Beach**
Istanbul has faced other attacks against non-Muslims recently. On Sunday, Jews were [attacked](https://www.jpost.com/diaspora/antisemitism/article-880950) on their way to light Hanukkah candles at Neve Shalom Synagogue. The synagogue has been the target of several other terrorist attacks, including

# Egypt, Turkey move in step as Washington, Jerusalem pull apart - analysis
## Regional coordination between Cairo and Ankara accelerates amid growing differences between the US and Israel over Turkey’s role after the Israel-Hamas War.
![Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan and his Egyptian counterpart Badr Abdelatty leave after a press conference in Ankara, Turkey, November 12, 2025.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/698839)Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan and his Egyptian counterpart Badr Abdelatty leave after a press conference in Ankara, Turkey, November 12, 2025.(photo credit: REUTERS/CAGLA GURDOGAN)ByWASEEM ABU MAHADI/THE MEDIA LINEDECEMBER 23, 2025 15:39Updated: DECEMBER 23, 2025 15:40
[CAIRO] In the aftermath of the Israel-Hamas War, US and Israeli officials are increasingly diverging in how they assess [Turkey’s](https://www.jpost.com/opinion/article-880969) regional role - a shift that has become more visible as [Egypt and Turkey](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-881040) deepen military and security coordination after years of estrangement.
For more stories from The Media Line go to 
The timing is specific. In recent days, US envoy Steve Witkoff has been preparing to meet in Miami with senior officials from [Egypt, Turkey, and Qatar](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-880832) to discuss next steps on Gaza, placing Ankara inside a diplomatic format focused on ceasefire implementation and postwar arrangements. The inclusion reflects Washington’s view that Turkey remains a necessary regional actor, even as Israel continues to view Ankara with deep suspicion.
That difference in approach now sits at the center of a growing US–Israel gap. While Washington has sought to widen the circle of regional partners involved in managing the postwar phase, Jerusalem has pressed to keep Turkey outside any framework that could shape [Gaza’s](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/article-881094) future security order.
This is not a public rupture, but a widening divergence over sequencing and control. US officials describe their approach as pragmatic, aimed at sustaining calm and preventing renewed escalation. Israeli officials see the same

# Turkey's Erdogan denounces Israel-Greece-Cyprus trilateral summit, affirms support for Gaza
## Turkish President Erdogan denounces the Israel-Greece-Cyprus meeting and vows Turkey will not allow violations of its rights in the Mediterranean, while continuing to support Gaza.
![Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan pictured in Ashgabat, Turkmenistan, December 12, 2025; illustrative.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/699088)Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan pictured in Ashgabat, Turkmenistan, December 12, 2025; illustrative.(photo credit: Contributor/Getty Images)By[JAMES GENN](https://www.jpost.com/Author/james-genn)DECEMBER 24, 2025 22:03
[Turkish](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-879705) President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Wednesday denounced the trilateral meeting between [Israel, Greece, and Cyprus](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/article-881024) earlier this week, discussing issues concerning security in the eastern Mediterranean, including the [Gaza Strip](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-881230).
Erdogan, speaking at a meeting of delegates from his Justice and Development Party (AKP) affirmed that [Turkey](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-880588) "will not allow violations of its rights in the Aegean and the Mediterranean."
"I am stating this very clearly and plainly… Whether in the eastern Mediterranean, in the Aegean, or anywhere else, we will neither accept injustice nor allow our rights to be trampled," Erdogan wrote on X/Twitter, summarizing his speech.
"Agreements can be made, signatures can be signed, orders can be placed, and various messages can be conveyed. None of these binds us or changes our policy. We did not fall for the game, and we will not. We did not succumb to provocations, and we will not," he added.
"We will not permit the violation of the rights and interests of Turkish Cypriots either," he continued.
![ \(L-R\) Recep Tayyip Erdogan and Benjamin Netanyahu seen over flags of Turkey and Israel, respectively \(illustrative\) \(credit: FLASH90/CANVA, REUTERS, SHUTTERSTOCK\)](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/621060) (L-R) Recep Tayyip

# Turkey
Turkey, officially the Republic of Turkey, is located partially in the continent of Asia and partially in Europe. It shares borders with Greece and Bulgaria to the Northwest, Iran to the east and Iraq to the southeast. It also shares various borders with Syria, Armenia, Azerbaijan and Georgia.
70%-75% of Turkey's population are Turks, and 19% are Kurds, making them the country's largest minority group. The country's capital is Ankara, and the largest city and financial city is Istanbul.
As of November 2021, the President of Turkey is Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, who has served in this position since 2014. Prior to that, he served as Prime Minister of the country from 2003-2014. 
Since the start of the Syrian civil war in 2011, an estimated 3.6 million Syrian refugees have taken up residence in Turkey, most of them living with temporary residence permits.

# Confirmed: Western Libyan Chief of Staff killed in plane crash
## The plane of the Chief of Staff of Western Libya, Mohammed al-Haddad, was lost after reporting an electronic malfunction, as it returned from Ankara.
[![Israel National News](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/000/1066138.jpg)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)
[Israel National News](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)


* * *
Published: Dec 23, 2025, 10:02 PM (GMT+2)
Updated: 22:26
[Turkey](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Turkey)[Libya](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Libya)[plane crash](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/plane_crash)
[![](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/vstatic/stripes/whatssappbanner-ENG.webp)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/joinwhatsapp)
![Western Libyan Chief of Staff Mohammed al-Haddad \(L\) with Turkish Defense Minister](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/781x439/1206480.jpg)Western Libyan Chief of Staff Mohammed al-Haddad (L) with Turkish Defense MinisterReuters
[](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/419721)[Jonathan Pollard’s harrowing testimony: 'They told me to kill myself'](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/419721 "Jonathan Pollard’s harrowing testimony: 'They told me to kill myself'")
Libyan Prime Minister Abdul Hamid Dbeibeh confirmed on Tuesday evening that his military's Chief of Staff, Mohammed al-Haddad, was killed in a plane crash while en route from Ankara, Turkey, to Tripoli.
Turkish media reports indicate that the plane's crew had previously reported an electronic malfunction and began preparations for an emergency landing. During the attempt, the plane drained its fuel tanks, and after that, all communication with the plane was lost. It was a private plane, headed by General al-Haddad, who was in Ankara for an official visit.
According to the report, the plane also carried the Chief of Staff’s advisor, Mohammed al-Assawi, Generals Al-Faitouri R'Ribiel and Mohammed Jumaa, as well as the accompanying individual, Mohammed al-Mahjoub.
General Mohammed al-Haddad is considered a key figure in the relationship between Turkey and the Western Libyan government operating out of Tripoli, which is facing the Eastern Libyan government that controls Benghazi and is supported by General Khalifa Haftar.
Al-Haddad has helped advance Turkey’s influence in Libya and Africa, particularly regarding Turkey’s claims of sovereignty over maritime areas in the Mediterranean, where it seeks to conduct gas exploration.

# The vacuum
## There is a strategic and political vacuum in Gaza. We should fill it now, before it is too late once again and we revert to the same failed policies of the last seventy years. Opinion.
[![Rabbi Steven Pruzansky, Esq.](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/000/1199203.jpg)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Rabbi_Steven_Pruzansky,_Esq.)
[Rabbi Steven Pruzansky, Esq.](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Rabbi_Steven_Pruzansky,_Esq.)
  * 2 minutes


* * *
Dec 25, 2025, 9:34 AM (GMT+2)
[Gaza Region](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Gaza_Region)[Rabbi Steven Pruzansky](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Rabbi_Steven_Pruzansky)
[![](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/vstatic/stripes/whatssappbanner-ENG.webp)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/joinwhatsapp)
![Netanyahu and Trump](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/781x439/1144662.jpg)Netanyahu and TrumpOfficial White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
If someone has a clue as to Israel’s current strategy in Gaza, please step forward. It is quite understandable if during the protracted conflict not much attention was paid to the day after. Nonetheless, there were certainly strategists and planners in our government that dealt with this and, presumably, the conduct of the war was designed to effectuate these long-term goals. But what are they?
The immediate war aims were articulated numerous times by our leadership: defeat of Hamas and its extinction as a military and political force in Gaza, including disarmament and exclusion from governance, and release of all the hostages. (Shame on our government for releasing all the terrorists - including murderers - before every last hostage, dead or alive, was returned to Israel.) 
Those aims were appropriate but we must note the distinction between eliminating Hamas and our strategy for post-war Gaza. Those are not the same. Even if Hamas is completely eradicated as a terrorist entity and political power - no easy task given the current war-weariness of the Americans, not to mention much of the Israeli public - eliminating a negative does not automatically create a positive. 
What then is our plan, not for Hamas, but for Gaza?
We have already missed several important opportunities. As politics abhors a vacuum, in place of our reticence a series of dreadful suggestions have been proffered, mainly involving rebuilding Gaza in the presence of foreign troops who can hardly be expected to challenge Hamas (if it survives) or thwart the rise of a new terrorist group under a different name. This is a bad plan based on fantasies - but a bad plan based on fantasies will always beat no plan at all.
It seems that we have reverted to the traditional Israeli craving for the status quo, kicking the can down the road, and hoping for the best - a few years of relative quiet. Those who thought that conceptziya was overwhelmed by the Hamas atrocities of October 7 should think again. That conceptziya is alive and well because it substitutes for politicians having to make tough decisions, including insisting on our just rights even in the face of American and international objections. 
According to reports, it has given rise to the obscenity that our soldiers are now engaged in clearing the ruins of Gaza under pressure from the United States, and at our expense. It is hard to believe that is true, but weirder things have happened. Is the IDF also being tasked with rebuilding the tunnels?
What should we want to happen in Gaza? It does not mean it will happen but if we do not propose it, it means we have forfeited any possibility of an ideal outcome. Id

# Netanyahu: 'Those thinking to re-establish their rule over us - forget it'
## Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, President of Cyprus Nikos Christodoulides, and Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis made a joint statement of cooperation this evening in the face of Turkish aggression.
[![Israel National News](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/000/1066138.jpg)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)
[Israel National News](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)

Dec 22, 2025, 7:28 PM (GMT+2)
[Greece](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Greece)[Israel-Greece](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Israel-Greece)[Cyprus-Israel](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Cyprus-Israel)[Cyprus](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Cyprus)
[![](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/vstatic/stripes/whatssappbanner-ENG.webp)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/joinwhatsapp)
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, President of Cyprus Nikos Christodoulides, and Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis made a joint statement this evening following a trilateral meeting in Jerusalem.
Prime Minister Netanyahu began: "This is not our first meeting. It's our tenth meeting. But I said in our discussions that I think it's the most consequential. The last time the three of us met was a short time before the October 7th massacre, before terror once again reminded us that stability in this region is never guaranteed, and that strength, clarity and cooperation are not options. They are a must."
"The Middle East and the Eastern Mediterranean are being tested by aggression, terrorism and instability. Our partnership provides strength, clarity and cooperation that will prevail over chaos. It also offers new opportunities for prosperity. We are cooperating, and discussed in detail cooperation, in many fields: energy, technology, connectivity and also security, that guarantees this all."
"We will advance IMEC, the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor. This is an idea that has been brought forward before, but we think we have to put it into reality. It's a maritime and rail pipeline, energy pipeline and cable connectivity between Asia and Europe going through Israel, Cyprus and Greece."
"As part of this effort, we are advancing the great sea connector that will enable electric energy cooperation between our three countries and Europe, and the Arabian Peninsula, and ultimately you can connect it to Asia as well. So, this is a big thing, and in many ways it's the skeleton of the IMEC corridor. We discussed practically how to move this forward, and we intend to advance it. I'll also discuss it in my upcoming meeting with President Trump in the United States."
"We agreed to deepen cooperation in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity and advanced research. Israel is already a global technology leader, and together with Greece and Cyprus, we will shape the region's future in cutting-edge new technologies. I want to tell you that a lot is going on right now. Israeli companies, hi-tech companies are flocking to Cyprus and to Greece. It basically says that they believe in the economic management. We're not jealous. We believe in competition."
"There are 186 flights a week between Israel and Cyprus. It's unbelievable. Just compute that. I don't know of many Israeli investors who have not visited Greece or who are not investing in Greece. Increasing amounts are growing there, and again, I think this is a sign of the dynamism of our econo

# Netanyahu sends Christmas greeting: Israel stands with Christians worldwide
## Netanyahu sends Christmas greetings to the Christian community, praising Israel as the only Middle Eastern country where Christians thrive freely, contrasting it with regional persecution and dwindling Christian communities.
[![Israel National News](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/000/1066138.jpg)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)
[Israel National News](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Israel_National_News)
  * 1 minutes


* * *
Dec 24, 2025, 11:38 PM (GMT+2)
[Christmas](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Christmas)[Benjamin Netanyahu](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Benjamin_Netanyahu)
[![](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/vstatic/stripes/whatssappbanner-ENG.webp)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/joinwhatsapp)
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday published a video with greetings for the Christian community ahead of the Christmas holiday.
“From Jerusalem, I send warm greetings to our Christian friends around the world. I wish you a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year. From here, the Holy Land, Israel, the only country in the Middle East where the Christian community is thriving,” said Netanyahu.
He added, “Israel is the only country in the Middle East where Christians can practice their faith with full rights and in total freedom, where Christian pilgrims are embraced with open arms and are so deeply appreciated, where Christians can celebrate proudly their traditions and openly do so without any fear.”
“In Jerusalem, the city municipality every year officially distributes Christmas trees. It's been doing so for two decades. By contrast, a few days ago, in the Palestinian town of Jenin, Palestinians burnt a Christmas tree in the Holy Redeemer Church,” continued Netanyahu.
He stressed, “That's the difference. Israel stands up for Christians across the region, wherever they face widespread intimidation and persecution. While Israel's Christian population is growing, the Christian population in countless areas across the region has been dwindling due to systematic discrimination and oppression. This has happened in Iraq. It's happened in Syria. It's happened in Lebanon. It's happened in Turkey. And it's happening in the Palestinian Authority.”
“The birthplace of Jesus, Bethlehem, used to have 80% Christian population. That's when we were there. When we left and gave it to the Palestinian Authority, it has since dwindled from 80% to 20%,” the Prime Minister said.
Related articles:
  * [While Israel protects Christmas, Jenin burns It](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/419806)
  * [Hamas supporters declared war on Christmas](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/382624)
  * ['If Jesus was born today, he'd be kidnapped by Hamas'](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/382645)
  * [Five arrested in foiled Islamist plot on Christmas market](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/news/419256)


“The persecution of Christians or members of any religion cannot and must not be tolerated. And Muslim militant displacement and attacks against Christians in Nigeria, that too must end. And it must end now,” Netanyahu stressed.
“I'm sending Israel's Christmas blessings to our Christian friends around the world. And I ask you, know that Israel will always stand with you,” he concluded.

# Erdoğan’s Turkey has declared war on Israel - and its Jews are paying the price.
## Human rights monitors have documented what Turkey’s Jews already feel daily: discrimination against religious minorities is no longer episodic but systemic. Opinion.
[![Stephen M. Flatow](https://2.a7.org/files/pictures/000/1199200.jpg)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Stephen_M._Flatow)
[Stephen M. Flatow](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/author/Stephen_M._Flatow)
  * 2 minutes


* * *
Dec 24, 2025, 7:15 AM (GMT+2)
[Israel-Turkey](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Israel-Turkey)[Stephen M. Flatow](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/tags/Stephen_M-_Flatow)
[![](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/vstatic/stripes/whatssappbanner-ENG.webp)](https://www.israelnationalnews.com/joinwhatsapp)

**Stephen M. Flatow** is _President of the Religious Zionists of America (RZA.) He is the father of Alisa Flatow, who was murdered in an Iranian-sponsored Palestinian terrorist attack in 1995 and the author of A Father’s Story: My Fight for Justice Against Iranian Terror now available in an expanded paperback edition on Amazon. Note: The RZA is not affiliated with any American or Israeli political party._
Turkey is no longer merely hostile to Israel. Under President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, it has embraced a campaign of incitement that is destabilizing the Middle East - and placing Turkey’s own Jewish citizens in growing danger.
For years, Ankara tried to posture as a regional power that could attack Israel rhetorically while preserving internal order. That illusion collapsed in 2025, as anti-Israel obsession hardened into street violence, legal persecution, and official policies that deliberately blur - and often erase - the distinction between Zionists, Israelis, and Jews.
The most disturbing proof came on December 21, when Jewish worshippers in Istanbul were attacked while walking to light Hanukkah candles at the Neve Shalom Synagogue. A hostile mob shouted, “we don’t want Zionists,” and demanded they leave the country. The incident reportedly followed online calls to protest the event because it was organized by a “pro-IDF” Jewish guide. In today’s Turkey, that label alone is enough to place Jews in physical danger.
This was not an isolated incident. Throughout 2025, mass anti-Israel demonstrations swept Turkey’s major cities, many marked by openly threatening rhetoric. Protests targeted Israeli-linked defense contractors and demanded harsher government action against the Jewish state. The U.S. Embassy in Ankara issued repeated security warnings - a remarkable step for a NATO ally and an unmistakable sign that the atmosphere Erdoğan has fostered is spinning beyond control.
Rather than tamping down the flames, the Turkish government has actively stoked them. Istanbul prosecutors issued symbolic arrest warrants for Israel’s prime minister and senior officials on genocide charges - legally meaningless but politically poisonous. Islamist parties allied with Erdoğan have proposed stripping Turkish citizenship from dual nationals who served in the Israel Defense Forces and confiscating their assets.
